title: Malaxis spiralis
intro: Malaxis spiralis, also known as the spiral adder's tongue orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis spiralis include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Malaxis spiralis is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Malaxis spiralis belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This genus Malaxis is also known as the "bald orchids" due to their often hairless or sparsely hairy leaves. Within the Orchidaceae family, Malaxis spiralis is further classified as a member of the subfamily Vanilloideae. The subfamily Vanilloideae is a group of orchids that are characterized by their often solitary flowers and a unique arrangement of their reproductive structures. As a member of the Vanilloideae subfamily, Malaxis spiralis is closely related to other orchids in this group.
